title: Reist
content:
{{Top|ffa|leg}} {{Character |type=Galactic Empire |image=[[File:Reist.jpg]] |name=Reist |homeworld=[[Droxine]]{{StoryCite|story=Death Masque|book=The Empire Strikes Back Monthly 149}} |birth= |death=[[3 ABY/Legends|3 ABY]], [[Lapez 3]] |species=[[Nightmare demon]] |gender=[[Gender/Legends|Male]]{{Fact}} |pronouns=He/him{{Fact}} |height= |mass= |hair=Bald |feathers= |eyes=White |skin= |cyber= |families= |parents= |partners= |siblings= |children= |affiliation=[[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]] |masters= |apprentices= }} {{Quote|Still, look on the bright side you little brute. If I wasn't here to keep you under control, you'd have to be exterminated!|Altin Wuho|}} '''Reist''' was a [[nightmare demon]] of [[Droxine]], one of the few to have survived his [[Species/Legends|species]]' [[Speciecide|extermination]] prior to the [[Clone Wars/Legends|Clone Wars]]. Able to use his [[Telepathy/Legends|telepathic]] powers to destroy his enemies, Reist was controlled by one of the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire's]] [[Human replica droid]]s, [[Altin Wuho]], who kept the nightmare demon contained under a [[Lead/Legends|lead]] hood to suppress his powers. Wuho also kept Reist contained in a floating sphere while aboard [[Captain/Legends|Captain]] [[Dirk Balor]]'s [[Dirk Balor's Star Destroyer|battle cruiser]]. While Balor was in the [[Lapez system/Legends|Lapez system]], the Imperial forces [[Skirmish on Lapez 3|encountered]] [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Alliance to Restore the Republic]] [[Pilot/Legends|pilot]] [[Luke Skywalker/Legends|Luke Skywalker]] and sought to destroy him. Reist was dispatched to destroy the young pilot, and was placed in a [[Homing missile/Legends|homing missile]] by Wuho, which was then fired at Skywalker's [[T-65 X-wing starfighter/Legends|T-65 X-wing starfighter]]. As the [[Missile/Legends|missile]] affixed itself to the [[Starfighter/Legends|fighter]], Skywalker was shot down over [[Lapez 3]] by a [[TIE/ln space superiority starfighter/Legends|TIE fighter]]. As the pilot landed, Reist entered his mind, creating all manner of nightmarish visions and apparitions. Just as Skywalker lost all hope, he was able to break through Reist's apparitions with the power of [[The Force/Legends|the Force]] and overcome the nightmare demon by striking him with his [[Lightsaber/Legends|lightsaber]]. While mortally wounded, Reist, with his [[Death/Legends|dying]] words, urged Skywalker to destroy Wuho. The young Rebel did so, before burying Reist on Lapez 3. ==Biography== ===Psychic killer=== {{Quote|''Reist'' is his name, Captain… Reist and I shall take care of Skywalker for you… or mostly just ''Reist''…|Altin Wuho|}} [[File:ReistBubble.jpg|thumb|left|Reist and his controller, Altin Wuho]] Reist was a [[nightmare demon]], a [[Species/Legends|species]] largely [[Speciecide|wiped out]] prior to the [[Clone Wars/Legends|Clone Wars]]. He possessed considerable [[Telepathy/Legends|telepathic]] powers, which allowed him to enter the minds of his adversaries and create all manner of visions and illusions. Through the illusions, he clamped himself to the minds of his victims and drained them of all hope, before proceeding to deprive them of their life. He was partnered with the [[Human replica droid]] [[Altin Wuho]], an agent of the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]]—Wuho was immune to Reist's telepathic powers, and by extension, the nightmare demon hated the [[Droid/Legends|droid]]. He was kept contained by a [[Lead/Legends|lead]] hood, which blocked his telepathic powers, and was furthermore kept in a floating, spherical bubble. Reist was also hated by members of the [[Imperial Military/Legends|Imperial Military]], who perceived him as a filthy animal. His existence was only permitted since Wuho kept his powers in check. In [[3 ABY/Legends|3 ABY]], Wuho and Reist were stationed aboard an [[Dirk Balor's Star Destroyer|Imperial battle cruiser]], which was in turn patrolling the [[Lapez system/Legends|Lapez system]]. When an [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Alliance to Restore the Republic]] [[T-65 X-wing starfighter/Legends|T-65 X-wing starfighter]] entered the [[Star system/Legends|system]], the battle cruiser's [[Captain/Legends|captain]], [[Dirk Balor]], deployed [[TIE/ln space superiority starfighter/Legends|TIE/LN starfighters]] to [[Skirmish on Lapez 3|engage]] it. Reist was brought to the [[Bridge (starship)/Legends|bridge]] with Wuho, who kept the nightmare demon contained. The Alliance [[Pilot/Legends|pilot]] displayed considerable skill, and the cruiser's [[Life-form scanner/Legends|long-range scanners]] revealed that the pilot had a [[Cybernetics/Legends|bionic hand]]. Balor immediately realized that the pilot was the infamous [[Luke Skywalker/Legends|Luke Skywalker]], a hero of the Rebellion. Although Balor was pleased that he would be able to capture Skywalker, Wuho insisted that he and Reist would be the ones to catch the pilot, although he admitted that the nightmare demon would be doing most of the work. ===Tormenting Luke Skywalker=== {{Quote|He's still ''alive''…''"
"''He won't be for long…|Captain Balor and Altin Wuho, on Luke Skywalker|}} Wuho took Reist away from the bridge and down to a special [[Hangar/Legends|launch bay]] where a specialized [[Homing missile/Legends|homing missile]] had been prepared. Taunting the nightmare demon, Wuho deactivated the containment bubble and placed Reist in the [[Missile/Legends|missile]]. The nightmare demon's hood was removed and he was sealed within the missile, which was then launched at Skywalker's X-wing [[Starfighter/Legends|fighter]]. As the Alliance pilot was busy outmaneuvering the TIE fighters, the missile secured itself against the fighter's fuselage. Skywalker was suddenly struck by a blast from one of the TIEs and forced to land on [[Lapez 3]]. As the pilot made his makeshift landing, Reist began conjuring images in his head. First, he made it appear as though Skywalker's landing was far worse than it was, projecting an image of a shattered [[Cockpit/Legends|cockpit]]. He then made Skywalker see Lapez 3 as a foreboding wasteland of hills shaped like skulls, and [[Tree/Legends|trees]] made from [[Skeleton/Legends|skeletons]]. In reality, it was a barren world, and Skywalker had landed relatively safely. He was unable to move from the cockpit, however, as Reist perched on his chest and continued to project illusions. Reist then showed Skywalker his own grave, suggesting that the pilot had not survived through the crash. The nightmare demon conjured up apparitions of two of Skywalker's friends, [[Han Solo/Legends|Han Solo]] and [[Chewbacca/Legends|Chewbacca]], before showing them being mercilessly gunned down by unseen assailants. As Skywalker reacted in horror, he was puzzled as to why Solo and Chewbacca were on Lapez 3. Reist heightened the verisimilitude of his vision by showing Solo's [[Freighter/Legends|freighter]], the [[Millennium Falcon/Legends|''Millennium Falcon'']], demolished and abandoned. Reist's next vision was of another of Skywalker's friends, [[Princess/Legends|Princess]] [[Leia Organa Solo/Legends|Leia Organa]], who he displayed being struck by the blast of a [[neutrino grenade]], before [[Death/Legends|dying]] in the young pilot's arms. Reist then simulated the rapid decomposition of Organa's corpse, turning the vision of her into a skeleton as Skywalker "held" her. Reist followed that up with an apparition of Skywalker's mortal enemy, the [[Dark Lord of the Sith/Legends|Dark Lord of the Sith]], [[Anakin Skywalker/Legends|Darth Vader]]. The nightmare demon's vision of Vader removed his mask, revealing a skull beneath, before lashing out and attacking Skywalker with his [[Lightsaber/Legends|lightsaber]]. Within the vision, Skywalker drew his own weapon, and [[Lightsaber duel/Legends|dueled]] the vision of Vader. ===Death of the Nightmare Demon=== {{Quote|Reist? ''Dead?'' That's impossible…|Altin Wuho|}} [[File:Reistdeath.jpg|thumb|right|250px|Reist is mortally wounded by Luke Skywalker.]] Skywalker, who felt he was losing to "Vader," fled the battle, and Reist conjured [[Stormtrooper/Legends|stormtroopers]] to pursue him. The Alliance pilot began to lose all hope, but was then able to use [[The Force/Legends|the Force]] to overcome Reist's illusions, and see reality once again. By this time, Reist was physically sitting on Skywalker's chest, and although the vision was shattered, the nightmare demon was still able to prevent the young man from moving a muscle. An [[Imperial shuttlecraft]] containing Wuho landed on the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] to retrieve Skywalker and Reist, but the young Alliance pilot was able to use the Force to overcome the powers of the nightmare demon. Reaching his lightsaber, Skywalker ignited the weapon, striking Reist in the chest and mortally wounding him. The nightmare demon fell from Skywalker's chest, out of the X-wing, and hit the Lapez 3 surface. As Reist lay dying, Skywalker took stock of his situation, and realized that his [[Starship/Legends|ship's]] [[C-37 computer relay]] had been blown in the crash landing, preventing him from leaving Lapez 3. Fearing that a [[Squad/Legends|squad]] of stormtroopers would emerge from the [[Shuttle/Legends|shuttlecraft]], Skywalker hid as Wuho emerged, alone, looking for Reist. As Skywalker attempted to hijack Wuho's shuttlecraft, Reist telepathically called out to the young man, hoping to take vengeance against Wuho. He mentioned that Wuho had a C-37 unit about his person, before finally expiring. Skywalker was then prompted to confront the droid. The two battled briefly, before Skywalker hurled his lightsaber into Wuho, destroying him instantly. The pilot retrieved the C-37 unit from Wuho's remains and repaired his ship. Before departing Lapez 3, he buried Reist's body in a makeshift grave, fashioning a tombstone with the nightmare demon's name upon it. ==Personality and traits== {{Quote|It is I, Reist. Taking vengeance at last on a master who has kept me imprisoned… a master I hate.|Reist|}} Reist was scorned and reviled by members of the Imperial Military, particularly Captain Balor. The nightmare demon was told by his master, the droid Wuho, that their hatred for him matched his own hatred for the droid. Reist was particularly vindictive as well, seeking punitive action against his master when he was mortally wounded by Luke Skywalker. He urged the young Alliance pilot to destroy Wuho in his dying moments, before finally succumbing to his wounds. ==Powers and abilities== {{Quote|You know I'm not going anywhere until you've got that beast ''hooded again!''|Captain Dirk Balor|}} Reist's considerable telepathic abilities allowed him to project images and visions into the minds of others, simulating totally fictional circumstances, environments, and individuals. He did this to control his victims, and when he held sway over them, he was able to prevent them from even being able to move in the real world. Despite this, his powers could be blocked by a lead hood. Reist was also able to communicate telepathically, talking directly to whomever he chose. The nightmare demon's powers were only broken by Luke Skywalker, a gifted user of the Force, who was able to break through the illusions and overcome Reist's hold on him. ==Behind the scenes== {{Quote|Sounds scary, but Reist was a monkey with a skull for a head.|Jason Fry, in "Comic Marvels"|{{InsiderCite|91|Comic Marvels}}}} Reist made his first and only appearance in [[The Empire Strikes Back Monthly 149|''The Empire Strikes Back Monthly'' 149]] ([[1981]]), a [[Star Wars comic books|comic]] story that was written by [[Steve Moore]] and illustrated by [[John Stokes]]. The character was also going to be profiled in the article ''[[Supernatural Encounters in the Star Wars Universe]]'', by [[Joseph Bongiorno|Joe Bongiorno]], which was meant to be published in ''[[Star Wars Gamer]]'' before the magazine was ultimately canceled.{{WebCite|url=http://home.earthlink.net/~kcfurman/rpg.html#supernaturalencounters|text=Star Wars Lostworlds|archivedate=20050830160143|nolive=1}} {{C|Archived at [[Wikipedia:Internet Archive|Archive.org]]}} In the article "[[Comic Marvels]]," published in [[Star Wars Insider 91|''Star Wars Insider'' 91]] ([[2006]]) and written by [[Jason Fry]], Reist was classified as an "Oddity" of the [[Marvel Comics]] [[Star Wars (1977)|''Star Wars'']] line. Fry later mentioned Reist, as well as referenced his odd status, in the 11th part of his endnotes for ''[[The Essential Guide to Warfare]]'', where he mentioned that Reist, [[Jaxxon/Legends|Jaxxon]], and [[Waru]] being the crew of the ''[[Lusankya/Legends|Lusankya]]'' would be the only way the scene of ''Lusankya'' emerging from Coruscant's depths could be any goofier. ==Appearances== *{{StoryCite|story=Death Masque|book=The Empire Strikes Back Monthly 149}} {{1st}} ==Sources== {{Mediacat|imagecat=Images of Reist}} *{{GalaxyCite|11|ComicScan|ComicScan: Paint Him Black}} *{{InsiderCite|91|Comic Marvels}} *''[[The Complete Star Wars Encyclopedia]]'' ==Notes and references== {{Reflist}} [[Category:Agents of the Galactic Empire]] [[Category:Males]] [[Category:Nightmare demons]]
